title: Hetaeria hylophiloides

taxonomy: Hetaeria hylophiloides is classified as a type of orchid, specifically belonging to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. It further falls under the genus Hetaeria, which comprises a small group of terrestrial orchids. The species name hylophiloides is derived from Greek words, indicating its affinity to forest environments. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Hetaeria hylophiloides shares characteristics with other terrestrial orchids, such as its ability to grow in shaded areas and obtain nutrients from the air. Hetaeria hylophiloides is native to Southeast Asia, where it is found growing in forests and woodland areas.
